page: Amazon Web Services r8idb.metal-96xl is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server instance powered by Intel x86_64 processors. It features 192 physical cores and 384 vCPUs with a dedicated allocation running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The system is equipped with 3072.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per vCPU.

This instance includes 22800 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage, offering high-capacity, low-latency local data access. It does not include any GPUs. For network connectivity, the server delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps.

Given its bare-metal architecture, massive memory capacity, and extensive local NVMe SSD storage, this instance represents a high-density resource profile. It is designed for workloads requiring direct hardware access, high memory-to-vCPU ratios, and rapid local storage I/O. Appropriate workloads include large-scale in-memory databases, big data analytics, enterprise-grade relational databases, and memory-intensive transactional processing systems.
description: Amazon Web Services r8idb.metal-96xl is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server configured with Intel x86_64 processors. It delivers 384 vCPUs across 192 physical cores with a dedicated CPU allocation running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The instance is equipped with 3072.0 GB of RAM, providing 8.0 GB of memory per vCPU, and features 22800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. There are no GPUs included on this platform. Networking capabilities feature a baseline bandwidth of 200 Gbps. This bare-metal configuration offers high resource density and direct hardware access, making it highly efficient for memory-intensive and storage-heavy enterprise workloads. It is optimized for large-scale in-memory databases, high-performance relational databases, big data analytics, and transactional processing applications.
